summary: Updated the repository's dtk-template-derived tooling. The PR bumped objdiff from v2.0.0-beta.6 to v2.0.0 and synchronized parts of tools/project.py with newer template behavior: safer flag string handling, visible regeneration messages for invalid/outdated config.json, ldflags support through the shared flag formatter, cleanup of objdiff compiler-map comments, and revised progress reporting/fancy progress logic. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the provided slice, so intent is inferred from the title and diff only.

key_files.reason: Bumped config.objdiff_tag from v2.0.0-beta.6 to v2.0.0.
Applied dtk-template script updates affecting flag formatting, config regeneration messages, ninja ldflags generation, objdiff compiler-map comments, and progress output.

what_changed: Changed config.objdiff_tag in configure.py from "v2.0.0-beta.6" to "v2.0.0".
Changed make_flags_str from accepting only Union[str, List[str]] cflags to accepting Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] flags.
make_flags_str now returns an empty string for None, joins lists with spaces, and otherwise returns the string value.
generate_build_ninja now emits the ldflags variable via make_flags_str(config.ldflags) instead of manually joining config.ldflags or an empty list.
When build config loading finds a missing config version, tools/project.py now prints "Invalid config.json, regenerating..." before deleting the file.
When build config loading finds an older config.json version than config.dtk_tag, tools/project.py now prints "Outdated config.json, regenerating..." before deleting the file.
Removed the comment saying some decomp.me compiler versions were not added yet, leaving the COMPILER_MAP entries directly documented by their mappings.
Simplified ProgressUnit state by removing per-unit cached fancy progress settings and objects_total, relying on config-level fancy settings and len(unit.objects).
Progress printing now reports file counts using len(unit.objects) rather than a separately maintained objects_total counter.
Fancy progress output was moved out of the per-unit progress loop and is now generated once for progress_units.get("all") or, if absent, progress_units.get("dol").

Fancy progress now reads config.progress_code_fancy_frac, config.progress_code_fancy_item, config.progress_data_fancy_frac, and config.progress_data_fancy_item at print time rather than storing copies on each ProgressUnit.
smart_moves: Centralizing flag conversion in make_flags_str avoids special-case ldflags handling and makes string/list/None config values behave consistently.
Printing explicit invalid/outdated config.json regeneration messages makes automatic cleanup less silent and easier to diagnose during local setup.
Replacing objects_total with len(unit.objects) removes duplicated state that could drift from the actual object set.
Emitting fancy progress once for the aggregate "all" or "dol" unit avoids repeated novelty output for every progress unit while preserving the overall milestone display.
Using the final objdiff v2.0.0 release instead of the beta aligns project tooling with a stable upstream tool tag.
decomp_lessons: Template sync PRs can include behavior changes beyond version bumps; review generated-tooling diffs for changes in local developer workflow such as config regeneration and progress output.
For build configuration fields that may be strings, lists, or omitted, use a shared normalizer rather than ad hoc joins at each call site.
Avoid maintaining count fields when the authoritative collection is already available; len(set_or_list) is safer than incrementing a parallel counter.
Progress or status output should distinguish detailed per-unit metrics from aggregate motivational/fancy output to avoid noisy logs.
When auto-deleting stale generated files, print why the regeneration is happening so future tool issues are easier to trace.
naming_conventions: Renamed the make_flags_str parameter from cflags to flags, reflecting that the helper now handles generic flags such as ldflags, not only compiler flags.
Continued using config.*_tag names for external tool version pins such as objdiff_tag, dtk_tag, binutils_tag, compilers_tag, sjiswrap_tag, and wibo_tag.
Progress configuration names remain grouped under config.progress_* for fancy progress controls.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching or C decompilation tactics were present; this PR is tooling/template maintenance.
Indirectly supports matching workflow by updating objdiff to v2.0.0 and keeping tools/project.py compatible with newer dtk-template behavior.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available; conclusions are based on the title and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that update dtk-template or tools/project.py to see additional template synchronization patterns.
Search for make_flags_str usage to confirm all flag-like config fields support string, list, and None values.
Search for progress_use_fancy changes to understand the expected aggregate progress unit naming, especially "all" versus "dol".
Check whether objdiff v2.0.0 required corresponding objdiff configuration schema changes in later PRs.
